title: Effectiveness of introducing point of care capillary testing and linking screening with routine appointments for increasing blood lead screening rates of young children: a before-after study.
ehr_evidence: Screening rates were determined from routinely collected service data
summary: A before-after study evaluating the effectiveness of introducing point-of-care capillary testing and linking blood lead screening with routine child health programs (like immunizations) to increase screening rates in children in Broken Hill, Australia.
key_findings: Annual screening rate increased from 0.39 in 2008 to 0.75 in 2012.
Greatest increases were observed in children aged 11–23 and 48–59 months.
Linking screening with well-accepted mainstream child health programs is an effective strategy to improve participation.
population: Pre-school aged children in Broken Hill, NSW, Australia
